notes: Desktop collapse regression fix. v1.6.31 added a 480px max-width to #app inside @media (min-width: 600px), but PHANTOM already had a pre-existing 768/1024/1400 adaptive layout system (`.page` padding bumps, KNOW/CLI/POWER multi-column grids, viewport-relative centering at 1400+). Those rules were authored when #app was viewport-wide; they stacked 96–136px of horizontal padding on top of the v1.6.31 clamp and tried to lay out 420-px-minimum grid columns inside a 480-px container, collapsing content to a thin central strip with one-word-per-line wrap on desktop. The work order's hypothesis (align-items:center shrink-fit) was incorrect — #app has no align-items override and cross-axis default stretch is honored; the real culprit was rule-stacking from the existing adaptive layout. Path A fix: add three overrides INSIDE the existing @media (min-width: 600px) block that defeat the 768/1024/1400 desktop rules via #app-prefixed specificity (1,1,0 vs 0,1,0/0,2,0). #app .page restores mobile-base padding. #app .app-header restores mobile-base horizontal padding. #app .fiber-sub/.cli-sub/.pwr-sub.stab-active reverts the multi-column grids to block flow. No JS touched, no onclick targets changed, no Bug 1/Bug 2 fixes from v1.6.31 disturbed. iPhone PWA byte-identical — all overrides live inside the desktop-only 600+ media query. SW cache bumped together.
